title: Vaccinium cereum
intro: Vaccinium cereum, commonly known as the white-berried bilberry, is a deciduous shrub that belongs to the heath family (Ericaceae). The health benefits of Vaccinium cereum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a valuable addition to traditional medicine. The bioactive constituents of this plant include anthocyanins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Vaccinium cereum is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, as well as being used in jams and preserves due to its sweet and slightly tart flavor.
taxonomy: Vaccinium cereum is classified within the family Ericaceae.
